steps: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/ and sign in with your Google account
Create a new project or select an existing one
Navigate to APIs & Services > Library and enable the 'Gmail API'
Go to APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen
Fill in the app name, user support email, and developer contact email, then click 'Save and Continue'
On the Scopes screen, add required Gmail scopes (e.g. .../auth/gmail.readonly for read, .../auth/gmail.send for send), then click 'Save and Continue'
Add your own Google account as a test user, then click 'Save and Continue'
Go to APIs & Services > Credentials at https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials
Select application type (e.g. 'Web application' or 'Desktop app')
Add authorized redirect URIs as needed, then click 'Create'
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the dialog

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: OAuth Client ID
credential_fields.description: Your Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 Client ID

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
credential_fields.description: Your Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 Client Secret

notes: Gmail API is free to use within quota limits (1 billion quota units per day)
Sensitive scopes (e.g. full mailbox access) require Google verification for apps used by external users beyond the test phase
External apps stay in 'Testing' mode with max 100 test users until Google verifies the app
The https://mail.google.com/ scope grants full mailbox access and is considered sensitive
For read-only access use .../auth/gmail.readonly; for sending use .../auth/gmail.send
Client secret can be re-downloaded as JSON from the Credentials page

test_endpoint.description: Returns the authenticated user's Gmail profile (email address, message counts)
